svg.text: GitHub Actions · Packer · CIS Hardening · Terraform · SSM · VPC Endpoints · OIDC
ci-cd.yml · triggered on push · OIDC credentials · all stages sequential
1. Every push triggers the GitHub Actions workflow.
2. Stage 1 runs static analysis: terraform fmt, tflint, checkov
on Terraform code; packer fmt and packer validate on the
Packer template. Any failure stops the pipeline.
3. Stage 2 runs packer build to bake the golden AMI.
The AMI ID is extracted from manifest.json.
4. Stage 3 runs terraform plan and terraform apply,
passing the AMI ID via -var. This deploys the test
EC2 instance in a private subnet with SSM access.
5. Stage 4 runs pytest, which executes CIS hardening
checks on the running instance via SSM SendCommand.
6. Stage 5 runs terraform destroy, deregisters the AMI,
and deletes the associated EBS snapshot.
Without cleanup, baked images accumulate silently.
SSH daemon hardening (root login, ciphers, MACs, KEX)
Disabled services (10 services masked, chronyd active)
Audit logging (auditd rules for identity, access, sudo)
Base OS: Amazon Linux 2023 · CIS Benchmark compliant
EC2 test instance (launched from baked AMI)
SSM over SSH: no inbound port, no key pair, no SSH security group rule.
The validation mechanism does not contradict the CIS hardening it verifies.
VPC Endpoints over NAT Gateway: private connectivity to SSM services
without any public internet exposure. Consistent with ITF Masters Tour.
OIDC over static keys: short-lived credentials scoped to the repository.
No long-lived secrets to rotate. Consistent with TerraDriftGuard.
Private subnet with no internet gateway: the test instance has no public IP
and no route to the internet. All connectivity is via VPC endpoints.
default_tags on Terraform provider: every resource tagged for Cost Explorer
without relying on individual modules. Packer uses tags and run_tags.
Single pipeline job: Terraform state stays on disk across all stages.
No artifact passing between runners. Teardown always has access to state.
IaC discipline: every resource managed by Terraform. No exceptions.
Lesson applied from TerraDriftGuard (resources surviving terraform destroy).
Test EC2 instance — 1 t3.micro in private subnet (minutes) .................................... <$0.01
VPC Endpoints — 3 interface endpoints (minutes of usage) ..................................... ~$0.03
Total: under $1 (all resources destroyed after evidence collection)
Project = "GoldenPipeline" tag applied to every resource for Cost Explorer filtering
GoldenPipeline — Designed and implemented by Malik Hamdane — 2026
